provenance.source_corpus: Effects of Emergent Vegetation Patterns on Flow Velocity, Turbulence, and Erosion Around River Banks

source_spans.text: The results reveal that vegetation patterns can significantly alter the distributions of flow velocity and TKE.
The results reveal that vegetation patterns can significantly alter the distributions of flow velocity and TKE.
The results reveal that vegetation patterns can significantly alter the distributions of flow velocity and TKE.
The location for the maximum TKE which may induce potential soil erosion for partly vegetated pattern moves forward 1 m in comparison with the vegetated pattern.

Gentler distributed flow velocity and lower TKE at the rear part of the vegetation section for partly vegetated pattern suggest that vegetation pattern is an important factor for aquatic vegetation recovery in rivers.
